reforms.description: Mexico has launched initiatives aimed at improving financial inclusion in marginalized communities, including the creation of the Banco Bienestar to provide access to financial services in remote areas and the Central Bank's platform Cobro Digital (CoDi) to facilitate digital payments, although the latter has faced limited adoption due to communication issues.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to enhance access to financial services, which can stimulate economic activity and improve quality of life in underserved areas.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives have been launched and are currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these initiatives are significant, they do not represent a structural overhaul of the financial system.
reforms.source_quote: Sembrando Vida aims at improving the quality of life of people in marginalized rural communities and promoting sustainable development.

reforms.description: Mexico established the National Policy for Financial Inclusion in 2020 to increase access to financial services, particularly for low-income households, and to promote financial literacy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This policy aims to enhance financial access and literacy, which can lead to increased economic participation and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy has been officially established and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This policy represents a significant structural change in how financial services are accessed and utilized in Mexico.
reforms.source_quote: Mexico established in 2011 the National Council for Financial Inclusion and in 2016 the National Policy for Financial Inclusion, then updated in 2020.

reforms.description: The Mexican government launched the 'Programa de Cobertura Social 2021-21' and the 'Internet para todos' programme to establish telecommunication networks and extend public digital infrastructure coverage, aiming to increase internet access for underserved populations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital infrastructure is essential for enhancing connectivity, which can drive economic growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Both programs have been launched and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: These programs are significant but do not represent a comprehensive overhaul of the digital infrastructure landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The programme 'Internet para todos' aims at extending the coverage of the public digital infrastructure from the current 70% to the whole country.

reforms.description: Mexico has created a digital identity document and a digital National Population Registry to streamline identification and verification processes for accessing digital financial products, enhancing consumer protection and reducing administrative burdens.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining identification processes can facilitate access to financial services and improve overall economic participation.
reforms.status_evidence: The digital identity document and registry have been established and are in use.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant advancement in the digital infrastructure for financial services.
reforms.source_quote: In 2020 the government created a digital identity document, to be used in all public administrations, and a digital National Population Registry.

reforms.description: The government simplified regulations on digital onboarding to include more institutions and facilitate remote account opening, enhancing access to financial services for a broader range of consumers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying regulations can lower barriers to entry for consumers, promoting greater financial inclusion and economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The simplification of regulations has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant but does not represent a major overhaul of the financial system.
reforms.source_quote: In 2020 the CNBV simplified the regulation on digital onboarding and extended its scope, by including corporates.
